name: Hyliidae
description: Hyliidae is a small family of African passerine birds with unclear taxonomic affinities. The two species inhabit tropical forests and are thought to be related to bush warblers and crombecs.

children.name: Hylia
children.description: A monotypic genus of warbler-like bird found in African rainforests.

children.name: Hylia prasina
children.description: The Green Hylia is a small, olive-green bird with a pale supercilium and a sharp, nasal call. It inhabits the canopy of lowland rainforest across west and central Africa, feeding on insects.

children.name: Pholidornis
children.description: A monotypic genus of tiny passerine resembling a tit, found in West and Central African forests.

children.name: Pholidornis rushiae
children.description: The Tit Hylia is a tiny bird with streaked brown and white plumage, resembling a small tit or warbler. It forages actively in the canopy of lowland forests for insects and small spiders.
